title: dryopteris rufescens
intro: Dryopteris rufescens, also known as the autumn fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory agent due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases, with its extracts exhibiting potential health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and enhancing immune function. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris rufescens can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and have been used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
